pattern: Extract individual digits by repeatedly taking the remainder mod 10, accumulate their product and sum, then return the difference.
hints: You need two running totals as you peel off digits one by one.
The last digit of n is n % 10; removing it is n // 10. Stop when n reaches zero.
Walk the digits once: multiply into a product accumulator, add into a sum accumulator, then return product minus sum.

edge_inputs.description: single-digit number, product equals sum so result is zero
contains digit 1, which does not change the product
digit zero makes the product zero
two-digit number where result is negative because product < sum is impossible with all-1 digits
